steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage claims John is a “hard worker” and “fully dedicated” to his job as an aguador. The decisive clue is the phrase “from early morn to the fall of the afternoon” in Choice C — "From early morn to the fall of the afternoon he would go from fountain to fountain and from portal to portal, his lean body so accustomed to bending that he never thought of straightening it, his head bowed as if in prayer.", which directly illustrates relentless, all-day labor.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice C — "From early morn to the fall of the afternoon he would go from fountain to fountain and from portal to portal, his lean body so accustomed to bending that he never thought of straightening it, his head bowed as if in prayer."’s description of John moving “from fountain to fountain” and his physical posture (“head bowed as if in prayer”) mirrors the claim’s emphasis on constant, dedicated work.

The other three either describe rewards (A), rules (B), or symbolic actions (D), none of which directly reflect continuous dedication.

That matches Choice C — "From early morn to the fall of the afternoon he would go from fountain to fountain and from portal to portal, his lean body so accustomed to bending that he never thought of straightening it, his head bowed as if in prayer.".
